channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Author George Saunders shares the experience he wishes he could give to every person
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author George Saunders talks about the experience he wishes he could give to every person.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author George Saunders talks about the experience he wishes he could give to every person.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: 'The Drama' is a half-baked comedy about wedding stress and cancel culture
item.description: Robert Pattinson and Zendaya play an engaged couple whose happiness is derailed when a boozy game of "What's the Worst Thing You've Ever Done?" uncovers a dark secret from the past.
item.content:encoded: Robert Pattinson and Zendaya play an engaged couple whose happiness is derailed when a boozy game of "What's the Worst Thing You've Ever Done?" uncovers a dark secret from the past.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Comic/filmmaker and star Julio Torres spins immigration stress into satire
item.description: Torres talks about the obstacles he faced in the immigration system after he came to the U.S. from El Salvador in his 20s. His new HBO Max show is Color Theories. Originally broadcast March 11, 2024.
item.content:encoded: Torres talks about the obstacles he faced in the immigration system after he came to the U.S. from El Salvador in his 20s. His new HBO Max show is Color Theories. Originally broadcast March 11, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Dark wedding comedy 'The Drama' has a provocative premise — but it never commits
item.description: Early on, Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new movie The Drama takes a turn. We're talking about it here: spoilers ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Early on, Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new movie The Drama takes a turn. We're talking about it here: spoilers ahead.

(Image credit: Courtesy of A24)
item.dc:creator: Aisha Harris

item.title: The surprising feminist history of baseball's biggest anthem
item.description: There's a lot more to "Take Me Out to the Ball Game" than "peanuts and Cracker Jack."
item.content:encoded:
There's a lot more to "Take Me Out to the Ball Game" than "peanuts and Cracker Jack."

(Image credit: Library of Congress)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni tossed out but robust case remains
item.description: Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni over the movie "It Ends With Us" were dismissed Thursday by a federal judge who left intact three claims, including retaliation, that will let a jury hear many of the allegations anyway.
item.content:encoded: Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni over the movie "It Ends With Us" were dismissed Thursday by a federal judge who left intact three claims, including retaliation, that will let a jury hear many of the allegations anyway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ziggy Stardust and Hacky Sack: What life was like the last time we went to the moon
item.description: The Artemis II mission is the first time humans have headed to the moon since 1972. That year also marked the debut of The Godfather and the Egg McMuffin.
item.content:encoded:
The Artemis II mission is the first time humans have headed to the moon since 1972. That year also marked the debut of The Godfather and the Egg McMuffin.

(Image credit: Evening Standard/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 'Stay Alive,' about daily life in Nazi Berlin, shows how easy it is to just go along
item.description: Historian Ian Buruma chronicles the lives of ordinary Berliners — including his own father — during World War II. Stay Alive is about the past, but has powerful lessons for the present.
item.content:encoded: Historian Ian Buruma chronicles the lives of ordinary Berliners — including his own father — during World War II. Stay Alive is about the past, but has powerful lessons for the present.
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: From 'The Old Man' to 'Giant,' John Lithgow is still going strong
item.description: Lithgow, 80, plays an intelligence agent in the FX action series The Old Man, and he's currently starring in the Broadway production of Giant, about a troubling side of children's author Roald Dahl.
item.content:encoded: Lithgow, 80, plays an intelligence agent in the FX action series The Old Man, and he's currently starring in the Broadway production of Giant, about a troubling side of children's author Roald Dahl.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: As hoops betting spikes, it's New Hampshire and other states vs. prediction markets
item.description: In New Hampshire and states with legalized sports gambling, wagering helps fund government services. But now competitors like Kalshi and Polymarket are getting a cut of the action.
item.content:encoded:
In New Hampshire and states with legalized sports gambling, wagering helps fund government services. But now competitors like Kalshi and Polymarket are getting a cut of the action.

(Image credit: Zoey Knox)
item.dc:creator: Todd Bookman
